seoTitle: How to Enable GIFs on Microsoft Teams Easily (2026)
metaDescription: Learn how to fix missing GIF options in Microsoft Teams by enabling connected experiences and checking admin messaging policies.
intro: When GIFs suddenly stop working in Teams, the fix usually lives in either your personal privacy settings or an organization-wide admin policy, depending on your account type. This guide covers turning on connected experiences in your personal settings, and for administrators, navigating the Teams Admin Center to enable Giphy content in messaging policies. It also covers a few troubleshooting steps for frozen or static previews.

steps.heading: Locate the GIF option
steps.body: Find the Emoji, GIFs, and Stickers icon inside the message compose box.

steps.heading: Check personal privacy settings
steps.body: Open your user settings, go to the privacy section, and toggle on optional connected experiences to allow external media loads.

steps.heading: Check OS accessibility settings
steps.body: Confirm your Windows or macOS accessibility settings have animation effects turned on, since disabling them can freeze GIF previews.

steps.heading: Check the admin messaging policy
steps.body: Administrators should log into the Microsoft 365 Admin Center, open the Teams admin dashboard, and go to messaging policies for the organization.

steps.heading: Enable Giphy and save
steps.body: Switch the Giphy in conversations toggle to On, set your preferred content rating, save the policy, and clear local app caches to sync the change.

faq.question: Why don't I see the GIF icon in Teams anymore?
faq.answer: This is usually tied to either a personal connected experiences setting being off, or an organization-wide messaging policy disabling Giphy content.

faq.question: How do administrators enable GIFs organization-wide?
faq.answer: In the Teams Admin Center, open messaging policies, select the organization's default profile, and switch the Giphy in conversations toggle to On.

faq.question: Can I control what kind of GIF content shows up?
faq.answer: Yes, admins can set custom content rating constraints to filter out unapproved media for workplace use.

faq.question: Why do my GIFs look frozen instead of animated?
faq.answer: This is often caused by operating system accessibility settings that disable animation effects, so check those settings on Windows or macOS.
